issues: The draft describes only the listings portion of the page, missing the more substantive Sockeyes game report ('Sockeyes roll over Kelowna') which is the primary Sockeyes-relevant article on the page.
The headline 'TODAY HOCKEY' is from the listings column, not the actual Sockeyes article headline, which is 'Sockeyes roll over Kelowna'.
The byline/credit 'Canadian Press' from the game report is omitted.
The short and long descriptions focus on the listings format rather than the Sockeyes game recap content, which includes specific scorers, quotes from Kurtenbach, and game context.
review.correctedDescriptionShort: Richmond Sockeyes defeated Kelowna Packers 7-5 to take a 3-0 series lead in the B.C. Junior Hockey League championship, with Dean Rutledge, Dave Tomlinson, and Jason Phillips each scoring twice.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: A Canadian Press game report on page 24 of The Vancouver Sun, March 21, 1987, recaps Richmond Sockeyes' 7-5 playoff victory over Kelowna Packers in Kelowna, giving Richmond a 3-0 lead in the best-of-nine BCJHL championship series. Richmond jumped to a 4-0 first-period lead; Dean Rutledge, Dave Tomlinson, and Jason Phillips each scored twice, with Mike McCormick adding a single. Coach Orland Kurtenbach credited intensity and physicality, while Kelowna coach Bill Hogaboam conceded his team was dominated. The page also includes a sports listings column noting the Richmond at Kelowna game 4 (8:00 p.m.) alongside NHL, Western League, soccer, and TV broadcast listings for the weekend.
review.notes: The page contains two Sockeyes-relevant items: a game-report article ('Sockeyes roll over Kelowna', Canadian Press) and a brief listing entry in the 'TODAY HOCKEY' column. The draft only described the listings. The game report is the primary article and should drive the headline, byline, and description.
